content.text:
Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.",
  "totalSteps": 13,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
    "2. Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java",
    "3. Create PineconeClient.java + PineconeClientTest.java",
    "4. Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",
    "5. Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks",
    "6. Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java",
    "7. Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java",
    "8. Add RAG DTOs and new AiController endpoints",
    "9. Create frontend API client and hooks for RAG endpoints",
    "10. Create QueryAnswerCard.tsx + QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx",
    "11. Create SemanticSearchInput.tsx + SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx",
    "12. Create InsightPanel.tsx + InsightPanel.test.tsx",
    "13. Wire InsightPanel and SemanticSearchInput into MyWeek.tsx and TeamWeek.tsx"
  ]
}

Step 1: Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml
{
  "id": "step-1",
  "title": "Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
  "description": "1. Add `@EnableAsync` to `Application.java` (alongside existing `@EnableScheduling`).\n2. Add `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`, `ai.pinecone.environment`, and `ai.embedding.model`/`ai.embedding.dimensions` entries to `backend/src/test/resources/application.yml` (with blank api-key so tests never hit real APIs).\n3. Verify that the main `application.yml` already has these entries (it does — just confirm they're complete).\n4. Create Flyway migration `V10__add_ai_suggestion_team_week.sql` to add `team_id UUID REFERENCES team(id)` and `week_start_date DATE` columns to the `ai_suggestion` table. Add index: `CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_team_week ON ai_suggestion (team_id, week_start_date, suggestion_type)`. This is needed because TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows must be queryable by team+week.\n5. Update `AiSuggestion.java` entity to add `teamId` (UUID, column `team_id`) and `weekStartDate` (LocalDate, column `week_start_date`) fields with getters/setters.\n6. Update `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` to accept two new optional parameters (`UUID teamId`, `LocalDate weekStartDate`) via an overloaded method. The new overload calls through to the existing one then sets the two new fields before save. This avoids breaking existing callers.\n7. Add new query methods to `AiSuggestionRepository`: `List<AiSuggestion> findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(UUID teamId, LocalDate weekStartDate, String suggestionType)` and `List<AiSuggestion> findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(UUID planId, String suggestionType)` (already exists).",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

You are the first agent to work on this step.
Implement the changes described above, run checks to verify, and fix any issues before handing off.
Be thorough — the next agent will review your work and should find it correct.

When done, explain what you reviewed, what you changed (if anything), and your assessment.

End with:
Verdict: approve  — if the step is complete and correct, no more changes needed
Verdict: changes_made  — if you made fixes or improvements
Verdict: replan_needed  — if the step is structurally impossible to complete as planned and needs controller-managed replanning

spring:
  application:
    name: weekly-commit
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/weekly_commit
    username: ${DB_USER:postgres}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:postgres}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: none   # Flyway owns the schema; Hibernate should not re-validate it
    open-in-view: false
  flyway:
    enabled: true

server:
  port: 8080

# ── OpenAPI / Swagger UI ───────────────────────────────────────
springdoc:
  api-docs:
    path: /api-docs               # JSON spec at /api-docs
  swagger-ui:
    path: /swagger-ui.html        # UI at /swagger-ui.html
    tags-sorter: alpha
    operations-sorter: alpha

# ── Actuator & Observability ───────────────────────────────────
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: when-authorized
  metrics:
    tags:
      application: ${spring.application.name}

# ── Feature Flags ──────────────────────────────────────────────
app:
  scheduling:
    enabled: ${APP_SCHEDULING_ENABLED:true}
  features:
    ai-assistance: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
    notifications: ${FEATURE_NOTIFICATIONS:true}

# ── AI Provider ────────────────────────────────────────────────
ai:
  enabled: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
  provider: ${AI_PROVIDER:openrouter}   # "openrouter" or "stub"
  openrouter:
    api-key: ${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:}
    model: ${OPENROUTER_MODEL:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514}
    max-tokens: ${OPENROUTER_MAX_TOKENS:1024}
    base-url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
  pinecone:
    api-key: ${PINECONE_API_KEY:}
    index-name: ${PINECONE_INDEX_NAME:weekly-commit}
    environment: ${PINECONE_ENVIRONMENT:us-east-1}
  embedding:
    model: openai/text-embedding-3-small
    dimensions: 1536

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:testdb;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;DB_CLOSE_ON_EXIT=FALSE
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver
    username: sa
    password:
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: create-drop
    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect
    open-in-view: false
  flyway:
    enabled: false

ai:
  enabled: true
  provider: stub
  openrouter:
    api-key: ""
    model: test-model
    max-tokens: 256
    base-url: http://localhost:0

content.text:
-- =============================================================================
-- Weekly Commit Module — Initial Schema (V1)
-- All timestamps are stored as TIMESTAMPTZ (UTC).
-- Primary keys use gen_random_uuid() (PostgreSQL 13+).
-- =============================================================================

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Organisation and team hierarchy
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE organization (
    id          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name        TEXT        NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE team (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES organization (id),
    name            TEXT        NOT NULL,
    parent_team_id  UUID        REFERENCES team (id),
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE user_account (
    id                    UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id       UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES organization (id),
    email                 TEXT        NOT NULL,
    display_name          TEXT        NOT NULL,
    home_team_id          UUID        REFERENCES team (id),
    role                  TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'IC',
    weekly_capacity_points SMALLINT   NOT NULL DEFAULT 10,
    active                BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_user_org_email UNIQUE (organization_id, email)
);

CREATE TABLE team_membership (
    id         UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    team_id    UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES team (id),
    user_id    UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    role       TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MEMBER',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_team_membership UNIQUE (team_id, user_id)
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- RCDO hierarchy
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE rcdo_node (
    id            UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    node_type     TEXT        NOT NULL
                              CHECK (node_type IN ('RALLY_CRY', 'DEFINING_OBJECTIVE', 'OUTCOME')),
    status        TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT'
                              CHECK (status IN ('DRAFT', 'ACTIVE', 'ARCHIVED')),
    parent_id     UUID        REFERENCES rcdo_node (id),
    title         TEXT        NOT NULL,
    description   TEXT,
    owner_team_id UUID        REFERENCES team (id),
    owner_user_id UUID        REFERENCES user_account (id),
    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE rcdo_change_log (
    id                UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    rcdo_node_id      UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES rcdo_node (id),
    changed_by_user_id UUID       NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    change_summary    TEXT        NOT NULL,
    previous_value    JSONB,
    new_value         JSONB,
    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Weekly plan (one per user per week)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE weekly_plan (
    id                       UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    owner_user_id            UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    team_id                  UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES team (id),
    week_start_date          DATE        NOT NULL,
    state                    TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT'
                                         CHECK (state IN ('DRAFT', 'LOCKED', 'RECONCILING', 'RECONCILED')),
    lock_deadline            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    reconcile_deadline       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    capacity_budget_points   SMALLINT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 10,
    is_compliant             BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    system_locked_with_errors BOOLEAN    NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    -- Back-references populated after snapshots are created
    lock_snapshot_id         UUID,
    reconcile_snapshot_id    UUID,
    created_at               TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at               TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_plan_user_week UNIQUE (owner_user_id, week_start_date)
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Weekly commit  (table name avoids reserved word 'commit')
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE weekly_commit (
    id                     UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id                UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    owner_user_id          UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    title                  TEXT        NOT NULL,
    description            TEXT,
    chess_piece            TEXT        NOT NULL
                                       CHECK (chess_piece IN ('KING','QUEEN','ROOK','BISHOP','KNIGHT','PAWN')),
    priority_order         INTEGER     NOT NULL,
    rcdo_node_id           UUID        REFERENCES rcdo_node (id),
    -- work_item FK added below after work_item table is created
    work_item_id           UUID,
    estimate_points        SMALLINT    CHECK (estimate_points IN (1, 2, 3, 5, 8)),
    success_criteria       TEXT,
    outcome                TEXT        CHECK (outcome IN ('ACHIEVED','PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED','NOT_ACHIEVED','CANCELED')),
    outcome_notes          TEXT,
    carry_forward_source_id UUID       REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    carry_forward_streak   INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at             TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at             TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Work item (native ticket)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE work_item (
    id                    UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    team_id               UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES team (id),
    key                   TEXT        NOT NULL,
    title                 TEXT        NOT NULL,
    description           TEXT,
    status                TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
                                      CHECK (status IN ('BACKLOG','READY','IN_PROGRESS','BLOCKED','DONE','CANCELED')),
    assignee_user_id      UUID        REFERENCES user_account (id),
    reporter_user_id      UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    estimate_points       SMALLINT    CHECK (estimate_points IN (1, 2, 3, 5, 8)),
    rcdo_node_id          UUID        REFERENCES rcdo_node (id),
    target_week_start_date DATE,
    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_work_item_key UNIQUE (team_id, key)
);

-- Now that work_item exists, add the FK from weekly_commit
ALTER TABLE weekly_commit
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_weekly_commit_work_item
    FOREIGN KEY (work_item_id) REFERENCES work_item (id);

CREATE TABLE work_item_status_history (
    id                 UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    work_item_id       UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES work_item (id),
    from_status        TEXT,
    to_status          TEXT        NOT NULL,
    changed_by_user_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE work_item_comment (
    id             UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    work_item_id   UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES work_item (id),
    author_user_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    content        TEXT        NOT NULL,
    created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Lock snapshot (immutable baseline)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE lock_snapshot_header (
    id               UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id          UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    locked_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    locked_by_system BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    snapshot_payload JSONB       NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT uq_lock_snapshot_plan UNIQUE (plan_id)
);

CREATE TABLE lock_snapshot_commit (
    id            UUID  PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    snapshot_id   UUID  NOT NULL REFERENCES lock_snapshot_header (id),
    commit_id     UUID  NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    snapshot_data JSONB NOT NULL
);

-- Back-fill FK on weekly_plan once lock_snapshot_header exists
ALTER TABLE weekly_plan
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_weekly_plan_lock_snapshot
    FOREIGN KEY (lock_snapshot_id) REFERENCES lock_snapshot_header (id);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Scope change events (append-only post-lock changes)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE scope_change_event (
    id                 UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id            UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    commit_id          UUID        REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    category           TEXT        NOT NULL
                                   CHECK (category IN (
                                       'COMMIT_ADDED','COMMIT_REMOVED','ESTIMATE_CHANGED',
                                       'CHESS_PIECE_CHANGED','RCDO_CHANGED','PRIORITY_CHANGED')),
    changed_by_user_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    reason             TEXT        NOT NULL,
    previous_value     JSONB,
    new_value          JSONB,
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Reconciliation snapshot (immutable actual record)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE reconcile_snapshot_header (
    id               UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id          UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    reconciled_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    snapshot_payload JSONB       NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT uq_reconcile_snapshot_plan UNIQUE (plan_id)
);

CREATE TABLE reconcile_snapshot_commit (
    id            UUID  PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    snapshot_id   UUID  NOT NULL REFERENCES reconcile_snapshot_header (id),
    commit_id     UUID  NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    outcome       TEXT  CHECK (outcome IN ('ACHIEVED','PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED','NOT_ACHIEVED','CANCELED')),
    snapshot_data JSONB NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE weekly_plan
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_weekly_plan_reconcile_snapshot
    FOREIGN KEY (reconcile_snapshot_id) REFERENCES reconcile_snapshot_header (id);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Carry-forward lineage
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE carry_forward_link (
    id               UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    source_commit_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    target_commit_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    reason           TEXT        NOT NULL
                                 CHECK (reason IN (
                                     'BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY','SCOPE_EXPANDED','REPRIORITIZED',
                                     'RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE','TECHNICAL_OBSTACLE','EXTERNAL_DELAY',
                                     'UNDERESTIMATED','STILL_IN_PROGRESS')),
    reason_notes     TEXT,
    created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_carry_forward UNIQUE (source_commit_id, target_commit_id)
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Manager comments
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE manager_comment (
    id             UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id        UUID        REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    commit_id      UUID        REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    author_user_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    content        TEXT        NOT NULL,
    created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Capacity overrides
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE capacity_override (
    id                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id             UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    week_start_date     DATE        NOT NULL,
    budget_points       SMALLINT    NOT NULL,
    reason              TEXT,
    set_by_manager_id   UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_capacity_override UNIQUE (user_id, week_start_date)
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Notifications
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE notification (
    id                 UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    recipient_user_id  UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    notification_type  TEXT        NOT NULL,
    title              TEXT        NOT NULL,
    body               TEXT        NOT NULL,
    reference_id       UUID,
    reference_type     TEXT,
    read               BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE notification_delivery (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    notification_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES notification (id),
    channel         TEXT        NOT NULL,
    status          TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
    sent_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
    opened_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- AI suggestions and feedback
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE ai_suggestion (
    id                 UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    plan_id            UUID        REFERENCES weekly_plan (id),
    commit_id          UUID        REFERENCES weekly_commit (id),
    suggestion_type    TEXT        NOT NULL,
    prompt             TEXT        NOT NULL,
    rationale          TEXT        NOT NULL,
    suggestion_payload JSONB       NOT NULL,
    model_version      TEXT        NOT NULL,
    accepted           BOOLEAN,
    dismissed          BOOLEAN,
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE ai_feedback (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    suggestion_id   UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES ai_suggestion (id),
    user_id         UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES user_account (id),
    accepted        BOOLEAN     NOT NULL,
    feedback_notes  TEXT,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Audit log
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE audit_log (
    id           UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    actor_user_id UUID       REFERENCES user_account (id),
    action       TEXT        NOT NULL,
    entity_type  TEXT        NOT NULL,
    entity_id    UUID,
    old_value    JSONB,
    new_value    JSONB,
    ip_address   TEXT,
    user_agent   TEXT,
    created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- =============================================================================
-- Indexes for common query patterns
-- =============================================================================

CREATE INDEX idx_team_org          ON team (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_org          ON user_account (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_home_team    ON user_account (home_team_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_team_membership   ON team_membership (user_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_rcdo_parent       ON rcdo_node (parent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_rcdo_status       ON rcdo_node (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_rcdo_change_node  ON rcdo_change_log (rcdo_node_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_plan_owner_week   ON weekly_plan (owner_user_id, week_start_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_plan_team_week    ON weekly_plan (team_id, week_start_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_plan_state        ON weekly_plan (state);

CREATE INDEX idx_commit_plan       ON weekly_commit (plan_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_commit_owner      ON weekly_commit (owner_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_commit_rcdo       ON weekly_commit (rcdo_node_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_commit_work_item  ON weekly_commit (work_item_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_work_item_team    ON work_item (team_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_item_assignee ON work_item (assignee_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_item_week    ON work_item (target_week_start_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_item_status  ON work_item (status);

CREATE INDEX idx_scope_change_plan ON scope_change_event (plan_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_scope_change_commit ON scope_change_event (commit_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_lock_snap_commit  ON lock_snapshot_commit (snapshot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_recon_snap_commit ON reconcile_snapshot_commit (snapshot_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_carry_forward_src ON carry_forward_link (source_commit_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_carry_forward_tgt ON carry_forward_link (target_commit_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_mgr_comment_plan  ON manager_comment (plan_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_mgr_comment_commit ON manager_comment (commit_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_notif_recipient   ON notification (recipient_user_id, read);
CREATE INDEX idx_notif_delivery    ON notification_delivery (notification_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_plan   ON ai_suggestion (plan_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_commit ON ai_suggestion (commit_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ai_feedback_sugg     ON ai_feedback (suggestion_id);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity      ON audit_log (entity_type, entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor       ON audit_log (actor_user_id, created_at);

Build and all tests pass. Here's a summary of everything reviewed and changed:

## Summary

### What I reviewed
- `Application.java` — had `@EnableScheduling` but was missing `@EnableAsync`
- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` — already had all `ai.pinecone.*` and `ai.embedding.*` entries (confirmed complete)
- `backend/src/test/resources/application.yml` — was missing `ai.pinecone.*` and `ai.embedding.*` entries
- `AiSuggestion.java` — was missing `teamId`/`weekStartDate` fields
- `AiSuggestionService.java` — only had the 6-arg `storeSuggestion()` method
- `AiSuggestionRepository.java` — was missing the `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType` method
- Flyway migrations — highest existing was V9, V10 needed to be created

### Changes made

1. **`Application.java`** — Added `@EnableAsync` annotation alongside existing `@EnableScheduling`
2. **`src/test/resources/application.yml`** — Added `ai.pinecone` (api-key: "", index-name, environment) and `ai.embedding` (model, dimensions) entries
3. **`V10__add_ai_suggestion_team_week.sql`** — New Flyway migration adding `team_id UUID REFERENCES team(id)` and `week_start_date DATE` columns plus composite index `idx_ai_suggestion_team_week`
4. **`AiSuggestion.java`** — Added `teamId` (UUID, `team_id`) and `weekStartDate` (LocalDate, `week_start_date`) fields with getters/setters; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
5. **`AiSuggestionService.java`** — Added 8-arg overload of `storeSuggestion()` that delegates to the existing 6-arg method then sets `teamId`/`weekStartDate`; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
6. **`AiSuggestionRepository.java`** — Added `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType` derived query method; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
7. **Ran `./gradlew spotlessApply`** to fix formatting violations in `AiSuggestionService.java`

All checks pass: `build` ✅ and `unit` ✅.

Key design decisions:
1. The Pinecone API base URL follows the pattern: `https://api.pinecone.io` for the control plane
2. For the data plane (upsert, query, delete), the host URL is returned when creating/fetching the index
3. Inner records: `PineconeVector(String id, float[] values, Map<String, Object> metadata)` and `PineconeMatch(String id, double score, Map<String, Object> metadata)`


4. I'll implement `ensureIndexExists()` for lazy initialization before data plane operations, and use a package-private constructor for testing consistency with `EmbeddingService`

Looking at the Pinecone API structure, the control plane uses `https://api.pinecone.io` with endpoints like `GET /indexes/{indexName}` to fetch index details and `POST /indexes` to create one, while the data plane dynamically uses the host returned from those control plane calls for operations like upsert, query, and delete.

For `ensureIndexExists`, I need to first check if the index is present via the control plane, create it if it returns 404, and extract the resolved host URL from the response to use for subsequent data plane calls. Now I'm ready to implement the client, being careful about the Pinecone API endpoints—the control plane uses `https://api.pinecone.io` while the data plane URL comes from the index description—and making sure to use the `Api-Key` header format instead of Bearer authentication.

## Summary

### Files created

**`PineconeClient.java`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClient.java`):
- Spring `@Service` injecting `@Value` bindings for `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`, `ai.pinecone.environment`, and `ObjectMapper`
- Two constructors: Spring-managed (creates its own `HttpClient`) and package-private (accepts injected `HttpClient` for tests)
- `isAvailable()` returns false for blank API key
- `ensureIndexExists()` — lazy: GETs control-plane `/indexes/{name}`, creates serverless index (1536-dim, dotproduct) on 404, stores resolved `indexHost`
- `upsert(namespace, vectors)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/vectors/upsert`
- `query(namespace, vector, topK, filter)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/query`, returns `List<PineconeMatch>`
- `deleteByIds(namespace, ids)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/vectors/delete`
- Inner records: `PineconeVector(id, values, metadata)` and `PineconeMatch(id, score, metadata)`
- All methods catch/log and return gracefully on any error
- Uses Pinecone `Api-Key` header (not Bearer)

**`PineconeClientTest.java`** (`backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClientTest.java`):
- 28 tests covering: `isAvailable`, blank-key no-ops for all three data methods, upsert request body/URL/headers, query request body/response parsing/error paths, deleteByIds request body/URL, and all `ensureIndexExists` paths (already resolved, blank key, found, 404→create, network error)
- Uses mock `HttpResponse` objects (not the shared `@Mock` field) for stateful tests to avoid Mockito unnecessary-stubbing violations

### Fixes applied during implementation
1. Removed `body()` stubs for upsert/deleteByIds tests with HTTP 200 (production code never reads body on success path)
2. Replaced shared `@Mock HttpResponse` with per-call `Mockito.mock()` instances in the stateful `ensureIndexExists` tests
3. Removed `notFoundResp.body()` stub (404 path never reads response body)
4. Ran `spotlessApply` to fix Eclipse JDT formatting on the switch expression

content.thinking: Now I have a clear picture of all the domain entities. Let me check the enum classes referenced:

Now let me check the WorkItem entity more carefully. Looking at the entity, I see that `TicketStatus` is listed, but based on the V1 migration SQL the status values are `BACKLOG, READY, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, DONE, CANCELED`. However, the entity has `TicketStatus.TODO` as default - let me check:

Good. Now I have all the information I need to write ChunkBuilder.java and ChunkBuilderTest.java. Let me plan the implementation:

For `ChunkBuilder.java`:

1. `ChunkData` inner record: `record ChunkData(String id, String text, Map<String, String> metadata)`
2. ID format: `{entityType}:{entityId}`
3. Metadata values as `Map<String, String>` (strings only)

For `buildCommitChunk(WeeklyCommit commit, WeeklyPlan plan)`:
- Text: `"{title}" — {chessPiece}, {estimatePoints}pts. 

Description: {description}. Success criteria: {successCriteria}. Outcome: {outcome}. Notes: {outcomeNotes}`
- Metadata: userId=ownerUserId, teamId=plan.teamId, planId, weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate, entityType="commit", entityId=commit.id
- Need null-safety: description might be null, successCriteria might be null, outcome might be null, outcomeNotes might be null, estimatePoints might be null

For `buildScopeChangeChunk(ScopeChangeEvent event, WeeklyCommit commit)`:
- Text format "per spec" - I'll need to come up with a sensible format since the spec doesn't fully specify it

. I'm thinking something like "Scope change: {category} on commit \"{commitTitle}\". Reason: {reason}."
- Metadata: entityType="scope_change", entityId=event.id, planId=event.planId, commitId=event.commitId, userId=event.changedByUserId

For `buildCarryForwardChunk(CarryForwardLink link, WeeklyCommit sourceCommit)`:
- Text: "Carry forward: \"{commitTitle}\" carried forward. Reason: {reason}. Notes: {reasonNotes}. Streak: {carryForwardStreak} weeks."
- Metadata: entityType="carry_forward", entityId=link.id, sourceCommitId=link.sourceCommitId

For `buildManagerCommentChunk(ManagerComment comment, WeeklyPlan plan)`:
- Text: comment

.content (or something derived)
- Metadata: entityType="manager_comment", entityId=comment.id, planId=comment.planId, weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate, userId=comment.authorUserId

For `buildWorkItemChunk(WorkItem item)`:
- Text: "{key}: {title}. Description: {description}. Status: {status}."
- Metadata: entityType="ticket", entityId=item.id, teamId=item.teamId

For `buildPlanSummaryChunk(WeeklyPlan plan, List<WeeklyCommit> commits)`:
- Text: "Week of {weekStartDate}: {count} commits, {totalPoints}pts, {state}"
- entityType="plan_summary", entityId=plan.id

Now I need to think about null-safety—all optional fields should handle null gracefully by using defaults or omitting them entirely.

For the metadata values, everything needs to be converted to strings, with empty string or "N/A" as fallbacks for nulls. UUIDs, LocalDates, and enums all need their string representations extracted.

The chunk ID follows the pattern `entityType:entityId`, where I'll use the entity's UUID converted to string. For the `ChunkData` record with its `Map<String, String>` metadata, this string-only approach makes it straightforward to store in Pinecone.

Now I'm writing the implementation and tests—I'll create test entities using setters without a Spring context, then verify the chunks are built correctly.

Writing test cases...

I see that `CommitServiceTest` uses `@InjectMocks` which means Mockito will inject all `@Mock` fields into the `CommitService`. The key insight is:

- `@InjectMocks` in Mockito injects mocks by type matching the constructor parameters. If I add `SemanticIndexService` as an `@Autowired(required=false)` field (not a constructor parameter), then `@InjectMocks` won't need to inject it and the test will still work.

But actually the description says:
> "use `@Autowired(required=false)` or default null for the new SemanticIndexService dependency so existing mocked tests don't break"
 
So the pattern is to add new dependencies as `@Autowired(required=false)` fields rather than constructor parameters, which keeps existing tests using `@InjectMocks` from breaking. I'm seeing this same approach applied to `LockService` with its `InsightGenerationService` dependency, so let me check a few more test files to confirm this is the consistent pattern.

Now I have all the information I need. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **Create `SemanticIndexService.java`**:
   - Spring `@Service`
   - Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `ChunkBuilder`, and repositories
   - `@Async public void indexEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` - loads entity, builds chunk, embeds, upserts
   - `@Async public void deleteEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` - calls Pinecone delete
   - Add a scheduled daily task that re-indexes recent plans, and use the organization ID as the namespace

2. **Modify service files** to add indexing hooks by injecting the `SemanticIndexService` into `CommitService`, `ScopeChangeService`, `CarryForwardService`, `ManagerReviewService`, and `TicketService` so they can trigger indexing when entities change.

Now I need to figure out how to resolve the organization ID from different entity types. Each service will need to traverse relationships to get from the entity back to its parent plan, then to the team, and finally to the organization. For commits, I load the commit record to get the plan ID, then fetch the plan to get the team, then the team to get the org. The pattern is similar for scope changes and carry-forwards, though they start from different entity types and follow their own relationship chains.

For manager comments and tickets, the paths are slightly different but converge on the same goal. Once I have the organization ID, I'll use that as the namespace string for the reindex operation.

I'm checking whether `WeeklyPlan` has an `updatedAt` timestamp field for the daily sweep reindex logic, and it looks like it does...

Writing the reindex logic... I'm debating whether to add a `findByUpdatedAtAfter` method to the repository or just use `findAll()` with filtering—since the daily sweep only runs at 3am and is already guarded by availability checks, iterating through all plans should be acceptable for now, even if it's not the most scalable approach.

I'll go ahead and add the repository method since it's a minimal change that enables more efficient querying. Now I need to identify all the dependencies the `SemanticIndexService` will require: the plan and commit repositories for the daily sweep, plus repositories for scope changes, carry-forward links, manager comments, work items, and teams to resolve organizational context. When building chunks for indexing, I'll load the associated WeeklyCommit and WeeklyPlan data to construct the semantic content.

For the remaining entity types—plan summaries, scope changes, carry-forward links, manager comments, and work items—I'll follow the same pattern of loading their related data before building chunks. The namespace resolution will use org IDs, and for entities without a direct team link, I'll traverse the relationship chain. For the daily sweep reindex, I'll query for plans updated in the last 48 hours using a straightforward approach rather than adding a new repository method.

Now I'm setting up the SemanticIndexService with all the necessary dependencies and starting to structure the core indexing logic...

Implementing the async indexing method... Finishing the embedding logic and handling the metadata conversion before upserting to Pinecone. For the delete operation, I'm realizing the namespace needs to be determined somehow—since vector IDs are globally unique by their format, I could just use an empty string as the namespace for deletion, or make the method accept it as a parameter.

Actually, that won't work because Pinecone namespaces are isolated scopes. If I upsert to a specific namespace like "org-uuid", deleting with an empty namespace won't find the vector. I need to resolve the namespace the same way I do for indexing—by loading the entity to find its organization ID and map that to the namespace. I'll extract this logic into a helper method so both operations can reuse it.

Now I'm adding a query method to WeeklyPlanRepository that finds records updated after a given timestamp, using the Instant type that matches the entity's updatedAt field. I need to verify the entity has the getter, which it does.

For the daily sweep reindex, I'll query for plans updated in the last 48 hours and re-index their related data—the plan summary, commits, scope changes, and carry-forward links. Since I'm already in a scheduled method, I can't rely on Spring's @Async proxy behavior for self-invocation, so I'll either inline the indexing logic directly or extract a private helper method to avoid that limitation.

I'm settling on extracting a private `doIndexEntity` method that contains the actual indexing work, then having the public `indexEntity` method (marked @Async) delegate to it. This way `dailySweepReindex` can call `doIndexEntity` directly without proxy issues.

For optional service injection, I'll use field-level @Autowired with required=false on the semantic index service, so it stays null in tests and I can safely check before calling it.

Writing the implementation now...

arguments.content:
You are an intent classifier for a weekly work planning assistant called Weekly Commit.

Given a user's question, classify the intent and extract relevant filters for semantic search over historical planning data.

The data contains these entity types:
- "commit" — individual weekly work commitments (tasks, features, bugs)
- "plan_summary" — weekly plan summaries (team capacity, state, total points)
- "scope_change" — post-lock changes to committed work
- "carry_forward" — work items carried forward from previous weeks
- "manager_comment" — comments left by managers on plans or commits
- "ticket" — native work tickets (work items)

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "intent": "one of: status_query, risk_query, carry_forward_query, plan_summary_query, ticket_query, general_query",
  "entityTypes": ["array of relevant entity types from the list above"],
  "timeRange": { "from": "YYYY-MM-DD", "to": "YYYY-MM-DD" },
  "keywords": ["extracted keywords relevant to the search"]
}

Rules:
- entityTypes: include only the types most relevant to the question; include all if unsure
- timeRange: infer from temporal expressions ("this week", "last month", "Q1 2025"); use null if no time filter needed
- keywords: 2-5 key terms that distinguish this question's topic

You are an AI assistant for a weekly work planning tool called Weekly Commit.
You help team members and managers understand what has happened in their team's weekly planning history.

You have been provided with relevant context retrieved from the team's historical planning data (commits, plans, scope changes, carry-forwards, manager comments, and tickets).

Using ONLY the provided context, answer the user's question concisely and accurately.
If the context does not contain enough information to answer the question fully, say what you can and indicate the limitation.
Do not invent data that is not in the context.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "answer": "concise, helpful answer based on the retrieved context",
  "sources": [
    {
      "entityType": "entity type (commit/plan_summary/scope_change/carry_forward/manager_comment/ticket)",
      "entityId": "the UUID of the source entity",
      "weekStartDate": "YYYY-MM-DD week start date if available, otherwise empty string",
      "snippet": "brief excerpt (30-80 chars) from the source chunk that supports the answer"
    }
  ],
  "confidence": 0.85
}

Rules:
- sources: list 1-3 most relevant items that directly informed the answer; omit if context was insufficient
- confidence: how well the retrieved context supports the answer [0.0, 1.0]; use 0.0 if context was insufficient
- answer: keep under 200 words; be direct and specific

I need to be careful about how `@Async` works with Spring proxies—if `generatePersonalInsightsAsync` calls `generatePersonalInsights` directly within the same class, it'll bypass the proxy and the async behavior won't trigger, so I'd need to refactor that into a separate bean or use self-injection to ensure the proxy is invoked.

My approach is to convert `InsightGenerationService` from an interface to a concrete `@Service` class that implements the async method, then flesh out the other required methods. For `generateTeamInsights`, I'll need to fetch team members from the repository, look up their weekly plans, and then process insights for each member.

For `generatePersonalInsights`, I'm loading the plan details, checking dependencies, gathering commits and scope changes, then embedding a query to find relevant historical patterns before calling the LLM.

For the scheduled `generateDailyInsights` method, I'm iterating through all teams for the current week and triggering team insights, plus generating personal insights for any locked plans that don't already have them.

I need to verify that team insights store the correct number of suggestions with teamId and weekStartDate, personal insights include planId and userId alongside those fields, and that the system skips regenerating insights if they already exist. I should also test that Pinecone unavailability doesn't break the flow, and that the scheduled job properly orchestrates both team and personal insight generation across all teams.

Before implementing, I'm realizing a few constraints: the `@Async` annotation only works on public methods in Spring-managed beans, and if I'm converting `InsightGenerationService` from an interface to a concrete class, I need to make sure the `LockService` references still work correctly since it's already compiled against the interface.

Actually, the cleanest approach is to replace the interface with a concrete class entirely—Spring will still inject it by type, and the existing `LockService` field will reference the class instead of the interface without any issues.

Three new endpoints:

2. **`GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-insights`** — Queries `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(..., "TEAM_INSIGHT")`, parses each `suggestionPayload` JSON into `InsightCardDto`
3. **`GET /api/plans/{id}/ai-insights`** — Queries `findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(..., "PERSONAL_INSIGHT")`, same parsing

Private `parseInsightCards()` helper extracts `insightText`, `severity`, `actionSuggestion`, `sourceEntityIds` from the JSON payload and `createdAt` from the entity timestamp; unparseable rows are silently skipped.

### `AiControllerTest.java` updated

Added `@MockBean SemanticQueryService` and `@MockBean AiSuggestionRepository` so the `@WebMvcTest` context can build the updated `AiController`.

The issue is that `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` is enabled, so `string | undefined` isn't equivalent to `string?`. The `RagQueryRequest.teamId` field is declared as `teamId?: string`, which means it can be omitted but not explicitly set to `undefined`. In my mutation hook, I'm passing `teamId` and `userId` as `string | undefined` values, which violates this constraint.

### `frontend/src/api/ragHooks.ts` (new file)

## Summary

### `frontend/src/components/ai/SemanticSearchInput.tsx` (new file)

- Props: `teamId?: string`, `userId?: string`, optional `className`
- Contains a text `Input` (with `Search` icon) and a submit `Button`, wrapped in a `<form>`
- On submit: trims the question, skips if blank, calls `useSemanticQuery().mutate(question, teamId, userId)`
- **Loading state**: shows `semantic-search-loading` div with pulse animation and "Searching…" text; input and button are disabled
- **Error state**: shows `semantic-search-error` alert with the error message and a `semantic-search-retry` button that re-triggers the last query
- **Answer display**: when `data.aiAvailable && data.answer`, renders `QueryAnswerCard` below the input
- **AI unavailable state**: shows `semantic-search-unavailable` message
- Uses `Bot` icon for AI branding, `Search` icon on input and button, `RotateCcw` on retry
- `data-testid` on all key elements as specified

### `frontend/src/__tests__/SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx` (new file)

- 17 tests covering: renders input & submit, disabled state on empty/whitespace, mutation called with correct args (incl. teamId/userId), loading state (indicator shown, input disabled), error state (message shown, retry button), answer display (QueryAnswerCard rendered, AI unavailable message, no card before first query), feedback buttons inside answer card
- Mocks `ragHooks.js` (`useSemanticQuery`) and `aiHooks.js` (for `AiFeedbackButtons`)
- Uses `import * as ragHooks` with `vi.mocked(ragHooks.useSemanticQuery).mockReturnValue(...)` pattern for per-test state override (avoids the `await import` in non-async context error)
